item.dc:creator: deafkitten

item.title: HR is not your friend. Your employer is not your family.
item.description: https://www.theguardian.com/money/2024/apr/03/human-resources-hr-departments-us-workers Just a friendly reminder that the Human Resources (HR) department works for the company and the company’s interests and not you own. Management may want you to think that you’re all a “family”, but family members don’t limit your sick time. And HR might be made up of some very nice people, who have to follow thick policies approved by management and you likely had to sign an agreement saying you would follow all the policies. If you find yourself in a position where your instinct is telling you that this “isn’t right”. It’s time to reach out. Here’s are a few ways to do that which can help you. For full disclosure, I have not and do not work in Human Resources or in any area closely related to such. Just years of experience working the corporate treadmill.
item.dc:creator: halfabubbleoff

item.title: Time for yet another unhinged XL Tuesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings, online friends
obviously
reasonable
that's a paddlin'
well it is pretty cringe
hersheys is best for smores, and thats about it
my god he's right
mmm hmm
counterpoint: no
that really is something
in my mind i'm always at Toyotathon
no thanks
it's about time
yeah!
ok
i thought this for so many years. tbh i still think it's the wrong one
::makes 'i don't know' sound::
that's my time. have a great night gang!!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Have a great Wednesday Imgur!
item.description:
I want one desperately.
Guilty.
Neil Gaiman and Sir Terry Pratchett tax because they bring me joy :) Edit: I think in #1 the beds are actually created that way for sustainability purposes as opposed to obstructing fun times, but it still gave me a giggle

item.title: Best Blind Date Ever!
item.description: On May 24, Amanda Seyfried teamed up with The Dodo and Muddy Paws Rescue in NYC for a new installment of 'Dodo Dream Date.' On her blind date with a shelter dog, Amanda just might have fallen in love!

item.title: Memes vol 296
item.description:
I have a whole printing tray dedicated to them and also every flat surface in my room
I almost threw up on my orthodontist when they did this
Ok but hear me out on baby Audrey
I’ve been happy for some time now- but I think I’m considered mid gen z cuz I’m turning 20 this year
Asterion and Pasiphaë
I used to groom dogs and I can only imagine shaving a FUCKING RACCOON I love them but that’s gotta be a really hard task
The one with his hand up his nose ( ,,◕ ̫ ◕,, )
It’s like that scene from not another teen movie where he’s like “you have your mothers eyes” and it’s a stick figure
Tugger my beloved
Froggy, Liza Jane, and Scruffy tax
Nessie tax

item.dc:creator: iamthecomet

item.title: Finally found a job
item.description: It's a contract position, but it is within my field of knowledge and not much of a pay cut from my old job. Let's see if it is a good fit, but for now, its time to learn an grow in a new job. Edit: This community is the best. Thank you all. The day went well, I followed the advice of all who commented, and I destroyed the bathroom to establish dominance, sang Rage Against the Machine to the staff, pelvic thrusted during the training video, asked about emotional support ferret policy, let a wild ferret in the building, and moved boxes in the warehouse using nothing but my underwear, safety shoes and safety glasses. Ty all and godspeed.
item.dc:creator: cafmaster

item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Unpopular opinion specimen
item.description:
My old ornithology professor hired me to taxidermy a puffin to donate to a local museum. (bird not killed for taxidermy! Collected dead during research)
Here they are with real grass
Here they are in a cubicle
Here they are in reverse
Here they are before plumpening and painting the feet and bill
This is how the bill would eventually look if not painted. These are decades old research specimens.
Here they are getting acupuncture
Here they are before hours and hours of grooming
Here they are as an icecube
Now, the intention was to gift this clown to another museum, but when returned to the old ornithologist, he fell in love and decided he couldn't part with it! Will need to find another for the museum. I am beyond flattered that a bird scientist absconded with the bird I taxied. Edit: glad you like it In case anyone else wants it https://imgflip.com/memegenerator/530920009/Unpopular-opinion-taxidermy-puffin
item.dc:creator: shandrono

item.title: Julia Morgan: "I Don't Think You Understand Just What My Work Here Has Been"
item.description: Julia Morgan Portrait: Public Domain Photo circa 1926 of US architect Julia Morgan. Created as a carte de visite. By Boyé Studio, San Francisco - https://digital.lib.calpoly.edu/rekl-2071, Public Domain, https://commons.wikimedia.org/w/index.php?curid=85460141 Sauce: Ginger Wadsworth, Julia Morgan: Architect of Dreams (Minneapolis, MN: Lerner Publications Company, 1990), p. 43. Anecdote retold in my own words to avoid plagiarism. “Julia Morgan (January 20, 1872 – February 2, 1957) was an American architect and engineer. She designed more than 700 buildings in California during a long and prolific career. She is best known for her work on Hearst Castle in San Simeon, California.” https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Julia_Morgan
item.dc:creator: oukid

item.title: You were the best you could be
item.description: The young man here is our son Austin. He was taken from our lives Sunday afternoon in a tragic accident through no fault of his own. He was 32 and a damn fine son, brother, husband, and father. He was a surveyor by trade and a good person by choice. Any way to describe him falls short. There is now one less good person on the planet. He would give you the shirt off his back, shoes off his feet, or the last $2 in his pocket. All he wanted was to be a better man. Son, you were the best you could be and we are proud to tell people about you. Our new normal is without you and damn that's gonna be tough. You were everything to all of us and your absence leaves a massive void in our lives that only your memory can fill.
item.dc:creator: RagingSlab

item.title: Screaming into the void (pls die in usersub)
item.description: She was emotionally abusive, required things her way, and alienated me from my friends. She had anger issues. It took way too long to break up with her. In large part because I do care, since she has abusive parents and I was trying to be a good example. Having a hero complex sucks. For her sake I'm trying to at least be friends. But today she wanted me back. Gave me a long-winded tale about how she's willing to change and "fix herself" and what went wrong. And how "dating is hard". But all I heard was desperation, manipulative tone, I'm not ready for anything in general, let along restarting with her. I've had nightmares about being together, reliving all our arguments. It's been almost a year since I broke up with her. I told her I can't be more than friends. That I'm not ready for anything with anyone. But she didn't take that as a no. Just a "not now." I had a panic attack and couldn't say more. Felt so uncomfortable I almost shit myself. When she asked I said "something isn't sitting well." That's all I could say. Besides, she won't understand how I feel. I tried that route before we broke up. I should be straight and say I can't and won't get back together. That I won't feel how I did in the past again. But I'm too soft. I'm not going back. No way. But I care too much to cut her completely off. We have not been intimate since months *before* the breakup. That would send the wrong message. I wouldn't even dream of such a thing. Hell, I have trouble keeping eye contact. But she won't get that message, or the fact that I'm intentionally not reaching out unless I have to. (Like when mail comes here that wasn't forwarded to her new address, which I don't have.) I'm going to have a hell of an appointment with my therapist next week. Please let this die in usersub. But I needed to scream into the void. It's helping a little bit.
item.dc:creator: throwawayvoid3742
